Title: A gene encoding an abscisic acid biosynthetic enzyme (LsNCED4) collocates with the high temperature germination locus Htg6.1 in lettuce (Lactuca sp.)

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cv. Salinas background; BC3S2 near-isogenic lines with UC96US23 introgression
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid cleavage step supplying xanthoxin for ABA production
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / upstream-to-mid ABA biosynthetic control point
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a major key control point for thermoinhibition-associated ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a direct precursor for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and contributes to thermoinhibition/dormancy responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Temperature regulated in imbibed seeds; higher expression associated with thermoinhibited seeds at warm temperatures
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In BC3S2 NIL seeds imbibed at 30–31°C, expression was 2- to 2.5-fold lower in lines carrying the UC96US23 allele than in lines carrying the Salinas allele
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High imbibition temperature / warm germination conditions (28–32°C; expression assayed after imbibition at 30–31°C)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL colocalization/fine mapping with Htg6.1; development of near-isogenic lines; marker-genotyped allele comparison; germination phenotyping across temperature gradients; qRT-PCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower LsNCED4 expression is associated with reduced thermoinhibition and increased high-temperature germination; the UC96US23 allele elevates maximum germination temperature by about 2–3°C
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LsNCED4 co-segregated with the maximum LOD peak of the major high-temperature germination QTL Htg6.1. 2. Near-isogenic lines homozygous for the UC96US23 allele showed markedly higher germination at 28–32°C than sister lines with the Salinas allele. 3. Seeds carrying the UC96US23 allele had 2- to 2.5-fold lower LsNCED4 expression after warm imbibition, implicating decreased ABA biosynthesis in improved thermotolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix lettuce GeneChip single position polymorphism (SPP) mapping microarray; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: UC96US23 NILs improved germination thermotolerance but did not reach the very high germination of the UC96US23 parent, indicating additional loci/background effects; no direct yield penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker-target candidate for breeding lettuce cultivars with improved high-temperature germination; introgression or expression-reducing alleles of LsNCED4 could be deployed, potentially combined with additional thermotolerance loci
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents strong candidate-gene evidence but notes that mutant/transgenic tests were still in progress, so causal proof was not yet fully conclusive.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cv. Salinas / accession UC96US23 mapping population
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Catabolic side-route controlling active GA pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2-oxidation and metabolic inactivation of active gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with/promotes depletion of hormones that support germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promote seed germination; reduced active GA suppresses germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher mRNA abundance in seeds imbibed in the dark than in the light; discussed from previous work cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with Gfr7.1 QTL affecting germination after far-red treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark imbibition and far-red light response at 20°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene mapping and QTL colocalization across environments; prior expression evidence cited in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to reduced germination in darkness/far-red conditions by increasing GA catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LsGA2ox1 co-segregated with Gfr7.1 QTL detected in two environments. 2. Gfr7.1 explained 21–22% of phenotypic variation for germination after far-red exposure. 3. The paper proposes that elevated LsGA2ox1 activity/expression in darkness may reduce active GA and suppress germination.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly tested; increased GA catabolism could suppress germination under some conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker for modifying light sensitivity of lettuce seed germination, likely in combination with other loci such as Gfr4.1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a collocating candidate gene with supportive but indirect evidence; not the main experimental focus of NIL validation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cv. Salinas / accession UC96US23 mapping population
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Described as regulating the committed step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC in the committed step of ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for ethylene affecting germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes or modulates seed germination responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene mapping relative to QTL interval
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mapped just outside the 2-LOD confidence interval for Ltg2.1, so not supported as the primary determinant of the LTG QTL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LsACS1 mapped near the Ltg2.1 QTL on LG2. 2. Because it lay just outside the 2-LOD confidence interval, the paper does not support it as the main causal gene for the LTG QTL. 3. It is discussed because ACC synthase controls the committed step of ethylene biosynthesis relevant to germination.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited, as evidence in this paper does not support primary causality for the mapped LTG QTL
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included due to direct QTL-proximal analysis in Results, but evidence is negative/weak compared with LsNCED4.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cv. Salinas / accession UC96US23 mapping population
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA suppresses seed germination and dormancy release
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: ABA signal transduction / positive mediation of ABA response
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Seed germination and dormancy regulation through ABA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ABA response pathway components downstream of SnRK2 kinase signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Expected to enhance ABA-responsive germination suppression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated candidate regulator collocating with Gfr4.1 for germination in darkness after far-red exposure
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; dark/far-red germination conditions inferred from trait context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Full-length lettuce homolog not cloned and expression not studied in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Germination in darkness at 20°C following far-red light exposure
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate-gene mapping and QTL colocalization; functional inference from Arabidopsis homolog literature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to enhance ABA action and suppress germination; collocates with Gfr4.1 QTL affecting far-red/dark germination
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A lettuce SnRK2.2 homolog co-segregated with Gfr4.1 on LG4. 2. Gfr4.1 was detected in two environments and affected germination after far-red exposure. 3. Based on Arabidopsis evidence, the paper proposes that increased SnRK2.2 function would enhance ABA signaling and suppress germination.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PP2C phosphatases; RCAR/PYR/PYL ABA receptors (discussed from literature, not directly validated here)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly tested; stronger ABA signaling may reduce germination under light-limited or stress conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/target for tuning lettuce seed light sensitivity and dormancy, but requires direct functional validation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in this paper is associative and based on colocalization rather than direct functional tests.
